author: @sumdumbmick
text: the problem with the idea that lithic industries are necessarily older than bone or wood industries is preservation. stone doesn't rot away like wood does, and facultative bone tools can easily also disappear completely or weather down to a point where they just look like random shattered bone fragments. so while the attested archaeological finds show a bias toward stone tools occurring much earlier it's not actually a fair conclusion to draw that they predate wood or bone tools.

text: "SGD Sacred Geometry Decoded" on YouTube has some great videos on more advanced and large-scale stone technologies, such as techniques for carving and moving massive megalithic structures, creating flat planes and 90 degree corners.  "Scientists Against Myth" is another channel that includes some great demonstrations of stone and copper technologies.

author: @ThomasAndersonbsf
text:
Heck a whole series on say "how to make a level, plumb/bob and from there a square," in the woods with just raw natural available stuff would be awesome, (basically using a body of water as your level or something to carry water in, and a bit of twine made from vines or strips of wood, bark ect to make a string, and a rock to tie on and hang down so its 90º angle from the level body of water, and it seems some compound that is liquid but solidifies would make a good flat body to build out from too, like sea shells for calcium carbonate and volcanic ash to get sulfur dioxide or some other source of sulfur to make calcium sulfate (plaster of paris) to make  a thin hydralic cement from ? XD 

(the applications in the shop as short cuts are awesome but I like to think it would be good that people knew these things should a great catastrophe should happen, we would not have lost it all like the info lost in say the libraries of alexadria I believe it was, basically making a "quick start for rebooting civilization" or something. Also could be something useful if one were to be stranded on an alien world that has only had biological and atmospheric terraforming done, before the castaways land there, something I thought would be awesome as a TV show where tehy actually work on developing tech as two different groups say out in the desert or something and get awards for achievements and such, as well as some value per day they last as they work to establish a civilization from scratch with just what would be in a ship they can salvage or something, :) Survivor: Space colony edition or some such ;)

author: @ThomasAndersonbsf
text: reason I brought up the idea of talking about south american tools is because of their obsidian swords as I would think those could be very effective saws on trees since it was a bunch of tooth like pieces of obsidian and all, :)

author: @ThomasAndersonbsf
text:
You should do a bit of a series on how various tools were made, what they were used for, (probably want to keep it mostly family friendly uses and let people look the other stuff up with their own discretion or something, and a minor warning or something that the other uses were brutal, or vulgar ect.) But how Obsidian was used and what all tools it was made into say in south american cultures would be quite interesting and probably lead to some different ways of looking at and approaching problems and the solutions they can offer as such? 

I am always quite fascinated at how amazing some of these things were and how ground breaking they could be. :)

(like imagine using flint knapping techniques to make a sharp tool from tungsten carbide cermet (ceramic metal to anyone reading and wondering) If you did not have the proper tools to shape it like diamond coating wheels and such, of high enough grit level to keep from ruining the edge once it is thin enough an angle and all that the grit ends up banging it too hard due to how large the individual grains of a grit are.  That was something I ran into a problem with for making special tooling from it for my lathe. I had to obtain much higher grades of grit of diamond material, and then I coat it with a wax like material so it keeps the tooling from snapping down between grits as it ride over the waxy material, though at slower speeds I am guessing an oil or water would work too, :) 

( I was also so surprised at how little effect silicon carbide had on tungsten carbide even thoguh it is so close to as hard as diamond, when compared to actual diamond granules for the grinding mechanism, as the SiC only polished the edge shining it up, but the diamond I can shape it almost like grinding into say plaster of paris with a stone grinding wheel to reshape things quite quickly, Very odd that. )

text: Once again, great video Dr. Smith. I've been knapping for years. Learned at the McNary Wildlife Refuge 20 years ago. I bury arrow heads near camp fires at camp sites for people to find. Always look back. Find the trail none went down. Please keep teaching..

author: @ebikeliverystable
text: The horn or antlers soon also provides a way in to shaping the Flints into shapes desired for other tools. . . But leaps and bounds to the use of shaping and moving those stones in Egypt. Ed Leedskalnin called it Magnicity. His experiments showed how the steel cable respond to the like force magnetism. Everyone brushes it off as kids experiment however if you upscale the cable to that of cranes sizes the experiment takes on a different light. He simply used his coils to direct the magnetism within those cables. The two coils of his PMH hold magnets within as he claimed. However a third coil placed at half the length of the steel cable then placed between those two and wired in properly it sends the North and South pole magnets out the opposite ends of the cable till the bar of the PMH is removed once charged with Magnicity/electricity (D.C. 24v more than likely) . Now the only real question to how or where he got his energy to power it all needs to be considered. What was sweetsixteen? Perhaps a earth battery garden of 16 cells. Even if each only gives 1.5v 16 of them would create 24v. Is there a male and a female battery? He also talks of plants and acidic value. Lots of studying in his WORD PUZZLE of writing style. To hard for some to have the patience for to read it. 😆. Took me MANY times of reading it to pull out whatever he has influenced my thoughts with. But he sure has a secret to share to anyone willing to listen to. 🕵👀🏆🖖
